post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 257
Road Sign Role Madness

If you're not currently alive in this game, do not post. (The game is over - anyone may now post.)
If you want to join a game, go to the Signups thread.
To learn how Mafia works, see the FAQ thread.


- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules for easy reference.

For experienced players: everything you need is here, and in the setup tab.
For new players: you may find it useful to familiarize yourself with the rest of the rules.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, OOT, gamethrow, link to external documents concerning this game, or use OGI. Further information about these topics can be found in the General tab and the Integrity and Etiquette tab.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote your role PM or any other private mod communication. You may claim your flavor, abilities, results, etc. - just don't directly quote a sentence from the mod (paraphrase instead!) or screenshot your role PM.
  • Do not discuss replacements. More specifically
    MU rules wrote:
    9a. Substitutions: Noting that a player X has subbed for player Y is fine. Noting when that occurred is fine. Discussing why someone has been subbed out, announcing you will sub out, telling someone to sub out, public speculation as to someone's alignment based on the substitution itself, all forbidden. Think what you will about this person's alignment, at your own risk. But using the substitution as reasoning is disallowed. It's bad for the game.

    To be clear, you can talk about the player who was subbed out. You can say that they were subbed out. You can say when they subbed out. You can say why the player that subbed out was scummy based on what they did or didn't do while they were a player. All that's being (not) discussed here is speculation as to why they subbed out. That's all the rule forbids. If you can be careful and stick to the legal stuff, you'll be fine. It's a very limited rule. The word "sub" is not lava, you can touch it. You don't get penalized for saying that a player was subbed in or out. Just don't discuss why they were subbed out. The "why" is what is ruled out. Think what you will, speculate internally. Just remember, the part that is illegal is "why they subbed out." It's not part of the game.

Activity
  • Each "Day" is 11 days. Each "Night" is 3 days. If all players agree to night end early, night will end early.
  • There is a 48 hour pregame period. Use this time to strategize, ask questions, etc.
  • Players may communicate in mafia chat and any other chats that may or may not exist at any time.
  • Each day there is majority, there will be a minimum deadline that will be announced at the start of the day. Day cannot end by majority hang until this minimum deadline has passed.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Be active. Activity rules are 48 hours to first PM prod, 72 hours to second PM prod, and 96 hours to replacement status.
  • If you would like to request replacement, privately tell the mods. Do not mention it in thread.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization does not matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Both bold vote and code vote formats are accepted, although bold is preferred. Refer to the Voting tab if you do not know what these are.
  • Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  • Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO. Ties are broken by whoever reached that number of votes first.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • Disagreements should be resolved in a way that does not affect gameplay.
  • Do not discuss rule violations by other players in the thread; contact the moderators privately.
  • Minor rule infractions will lead to warnings. Repeated minor rule infractions will lead to replacement.
  • Players may be replaced at the moderators' discretion for procedural difficulties.
  • All other rules are standard.

Setup and Moderators
  • The setup is closed role madness. For more information, see the Setup tab.
  • This game is moderated by chess24 and BobDBuilder321
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. Contact the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contact us through your role PM or through Discord (chess24#4527).
  • For a list of basic resources, refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For a more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, go here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate; access to spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as appropriate.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format to be counted: Vote: [player username].
    • By default, votes should be made in [b] (bold) tags. However, votes made in [code] and [c] tags will also be accepted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is similarly formatted, even with separate tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Votecounts will be posted periodically. Players may request a new VC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Invalid votes that appear real may or may not be counted.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • Votes will be carried over if a player is replaced.
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts another part of the votecount (VC).
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect VC will become official.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • "Day" starts at 11 days.
    • Each "Night" is 3 days. This may eventually be shortened. If all players agree to night end early, night will end early.
    • There is a 48 hour pregame period. Use this time to strategize, ask questions, etc.
    • Each day there is majority, there will be a minimum deadline that will be announced at the start of the day. Day cannot end by majority hang until this minimum deadline has passed.
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1).
    • If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game should request replacement by posting Request Replacement in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 96 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators.
    • Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves.
    • Do not spam.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
      Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion.
    • Minor rule infractions will lead to warnings. Repeated rule infractions will lead to replacement.

    The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, click here.

  • - Moderation Policies -
  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • chess24 and BobDBuilder321 are the moderators of Mafia Game 257.
    • Credit to fdas for reviewing the setup, and credit to BobDBuilder321 for events and feedback on the setup.
    • Credit to mathjay, Mathletesv, HannahW, and others for OCRSTM (this ruleset) and management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG[NUMBER] OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) 10.
    Current version: 10.0.1
    Version History

    OCRSTM 10 is available for public use. If you are interested in using OCRSTM in your own games, click here.
Setup
Credit to fdas for reviewing the setup, and credit to BobDBuilder321 for events and feedback on the setup.

- 13er: 10 Town, 3 Mafia.
- Closed Role Madness, everyone is a PR.
- There are events.
- This game is not bastard, though elements that might be considered borderline bastard may exist.
- For example, there may be nonstandard roles (such as redirectors) that may mess with your actions - for this game's purposes, anything influenced by said roles will not be considered as lying. It is guaranteed, however, that Framers, Godfather, Millers, or other roles of that sort do not exist, nor are there alignment conversion or any borderline bastard mechanics of that sort.
- Any investigative abilities will show their target(s) in their results.
- Unless otherwise specified, players are by default multitasking, non-consecutive, and non self-targeting.
- Actions will be processed in accordance with Reasonable Action Resolution.
- Mafia have been provided with fakeclaims.
- Do not quote or pretend to quote your role PM or any other private mod communication. You may claim your flavor (flavor is not AI), abilities, results, etc. - just don't directly quote a sentence from the mod, paraphrase instead.
- When signing up, players had the option to request a higher chance of randing a more complex role. Players should not discuss this.
- Flips are full role PMs by default.
- Town win when all Mafia are eliminated. Mafia win upon reaching parity, or when nothing can prevent this.
- Mafia have access to the following factional abilities:
Factional Actions wrote:
Nightkill: Compulsive Night Action - Assigned
- Kill a player. (Note: Unlike with other actions, you may use this on the same player multiple times in a row.)

Backup: Immediate Action
- Choose a dead Mafia member. Gain a factional 1-shot copy of each of their abilities (they can be used by any wolf).
- This may only be used once.
Mafia may or may not have other factional abilities.

Note: Inspiration for this game has been drawn from many sources, most notably offsite mashes. For example, the "request a more complex role" comes from this game. Credit to that game and many others for making this one possible.

[*]Votes will be carried over if a player is replaced. 
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts another part of the votecount (VC). 
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect VC will become official.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged. 
[*]Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]
 
 
[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*][b]"Day" starts at 11 days[/b].
[*]Each [b]"Night" is 3 days[/b]. This may eventually be shortened. If all players agree to night end early, night will end early.
[*]There is a 48 hour pregame period. Use this time to strategize, ask questions, etc.
[*]Each day there is majority, there will be a [b]minimum deadline[/b] that will be announced at the start of the day. Day cannot end by majority hang until this minimum deadline has passed.
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1). 
[*]If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance.
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game should request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 96 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM.
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]
 
 
[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.[/list]
 
The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617334]click here[/url]. 
 
 
[list][*]Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves. 
[*]Do not spam.
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
[*]Be mature and use common sense.
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
[b]Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion.[/b]
[*]Minor rule infractions will lead to warnings. Repeated rule infractions will lead to replacement.[/list]
 
The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617342]click here[/url].[/hide]
 
[*][b][url=https://aops.com/community/p24617357]- Moderation Policies -[/url][/b]
 
[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]
 
 
[list][*]chess24 and BobDBuilder321 are the moderators of Mafia Game 257.
[*]Credit to fdas for reviewing the setup, and credit to BobDBuilder321 for events and feedback on the setup.
[*]Credit to mathjay, Mathletesv, HannahW, and others for OCRSTM (this ruleset) and management of the private chat discord system.
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.[/list]
 
[b]MG[NUMBER] OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset[sup]TM[/sup] (OCRSTM) 10.[/b]
[i]Current version: 10.0.1[/i]
[url=https://aops.com/community/p21335350]Version History[/url]
 
[b]OCRSTM 10 is available for public use. If you are interested in using OCRSTM in your own games, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24637777]click here[/url]. [/b]
[/hide][/list]
[hide=Setup]
[i]Credit to fdas for reviewing the setup, and credit to BobDBuilder321 for events and feedback on the setup.[/i]

- 13er: 10 Town, 3 Mafia.
- Closed Role Madness, everyone is a PR.
- There are events.
- This game is not bastard, though elements that might be considered borderline bastard may exist.
- For example, there may be nonstandard roles (such as redirectors) that may mess with your actions - for this game's purposes, anything influenced by said roles will not be considered as lying. It is guaranteed, however, that Framers, Godfather, Millers, or other roles of that sort do not exist, nor are there alignment conversion or any borderline bastard mechanics of that sort.
- Any investigative abilities will show their target(s) in their results.
- Unless otherwise specified, players are by default multitasking, non-consecutive, and non self-targeting.
- Actions will be processed in accordance with [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Reasonable_Action_Resolution]Reasonable Action Resolution[/url].
- Mafia have been provided with fakeclaims.
- Do not quote or pretend to quote your role PM or any other private mod communication. You may claim your flavor (flavor is not AI), abilities, results, etc. - just don't directly quote a sentence from the mod, paraphrase instead.
- When signing up, players had the option to request a higher chance of randing a more complex role. Players should not discuss this.
- Flips are full role PMs by default.
- Town win when all Mafia are eliminated. Mafia win upon reaching parity, or when nothing can prevent this.
- Mafia have access to the following factional abilities:
[quote=Factional Actions]
[b]Nightkill: Compulsive Night Action[/b] - Assigned
- Kill a player. (Note: Unlike with other actions, you may use this on the same player multiple times in a row.)

[b]Backup: Immediate Action[/b]
- Choose a dead Mafia member. Gain a factional 1-shot copy of each of their abilities (they can be used by any wolf).
- This may only be used once.
[/quote]
Mafia may or may not have other factional abilities.

Note: Inspiration for this game has been drawn from many sources, most notably offsite mashes. For example, the "request a more complex role" comes from [url=https://www.mafiauniverse.com/forums/threads/33544-Money-Heist-Semi-Anonymous-Mish-Mash]this game[/url]. Credit to that game and many others for making this one possible.
[/hide]

post_rendered:
Event: SHEEP

The goal of the game is to get the most points.

To get points, you must submit (in your PM) answers to the questions below. The number of points you get per question is equal to the number of people who submitted the same answer.

All answers are accepted - nothing is "wrong," but remember that you're trying to choose an answer other people will also submit.

The first submission is final.

Similar answers will be grouped.

Research is allowed.

You may not talk about the event anywhere (including private chats), except in your PM with the mods.

Every player's submissions and the number of points they got will be publicly revealed.

Submissions are due 48 hours before EoD - Tuesday, 9/6 at 5:00 PM ET.

Questions
1. Name a road sign with an odd number of sides.
2. Name a road sign with an even number of sides.
3. Name a textless road sign.
4. Name a traffic light color.
5. Name a traffic violation.
6. Name a road sign color.

To hopefully clear stuff up: I usually read the “” emoji like a /j /hj or /nsrs or something along those lines to imply that the preceding statement should not be taken seriously

That’s mainly because I usually see it after joking text in the place where a tone tag might go

To me it looked like Chris was almost certainly using the smile to show some jokingness/not seriousness and I used the same smile to imply the same not serious tone (in the same way I would put a /j tag at the end of my post if I was joking with a friend and they were putting /j at the end of their posts)

To Kroger... for milk that your dad never brought back.



Rules:
- All players must publicly submit a route in bold that goes from A5 to the Kroger at D1. You can reduce your chances of your submission being missed by PMing us the link.

- Players may communicate about this event anywhere, including in mod-given chats.

It's been going on for a long time, no counter wagons, no opposition, so either joe is scum and her scumpartners aren't doing a good job of saving her (other possibility is MM + deepwolf because MM has been defending joe and a potential snow, ND deepwolf doesn't defend joe here I think) OR joe is town and scum are just frolicking their way to an easy mishang.

post_rendered:
Hi guys I was out of service the whole weekend and haven't done much thinking about this game.. sad that ND is gone. There's also no reason to assume I was docced and I wouldn't speculate about that too much right now.. But I'm also not sure when is the best time to start talking about roles/night actions. The last time I played role madness was 2.5 years ago and I replaced in on day 4 and me and tikki just powerwolfed the whole time. As of right now I would assume that ND was the mafia kill

As for my "doubts" on snow.. those were largely an "if joe flips scum" thing since they had a lot of w/w equity with joe. I still think she is held too highly in the general consensus, as I think people are giving her a pass without actually analyzing her that much. But, voting wisteria at eod yesterday was towny I think, and she generally didn't seem to have an agenda yesterday.. I would expect some sort of plan out of an experienced player playing scum. (and btw joe if ur still reading this I'm sorry about yesterday.. I've been mishanged d1/d2 more times than I can count and it sucks).

As for who is scum.. I think Wisteria is a top candidate fs (bandwagon onto joe, didn't do much else yesterday). Would look into bigmath. I would also like to interact with ad/snow a lot more today, to get more of a town structure going, or if one or both is scum, to figure that out sooner rather than later.
Getting cai, cheesecake voted out I would not be opposed to, BUT unless they actually show up we shouldn't consider them in the "hang pool" until like the very end of the day.. Spending the whole day playing SBPC is gonna get us all screwed over.

- Target a player, killing them.
- You will only successfully target the player if they have been visited at least 3 other times this game, including this night (however, this action will not be counted towards that total until the next cycle, if successful).
- If the target has been visited 6 or more times, this kill will become unstoppable.
- This ability can only be used on even nights.
- Once per game, you will automatically be refunded a shot to be used the next night (ignoring the above restriction), if a shot from this action does not go through due to not meeting its conditions. This refunded shot must satisfy the same conditions.

[b]Alerted: Passive[/b]
- After 5 Town die, the requirement for [b]Crush[/b] to succeed increase by 1 visit.

Event: MS Paint

Acceptable Submissions: Road, Road Sign, Intersection.

Using MS Paint, or some tool that produces drawings with a similar "feel", draw one of the possible submissions listed above, and submit it in your PM.

The first submission is final.

You may not talk about the event anywhere (including private chats), except in your PM with the mods.

Once submissions are compiled, they will be publicly posted and players will vote for their favorite ones.

Players are required to submit a first and second place vote. They may not vote for themselves.

1st place votes will earn the drawer 2 points, and 2nd place votes will earn them 1 point. After points are compiled, the player with the most points wins.

The identities of the drawers and how many points they received will be revealed.

Your paint submission is due in 3 days (subject to change). Your votes will be due 24 hours before deadline, or whenever hammer occurs. Results will be published at EoD.

Diary Entry
N2, G257
Dear Diary,

School was canceled. I don't think you want to hear about what I did today. It was kind of boring. But I will tell you about what I think in the Mafia Game I am playing in.

From SHEEP, I got to postpone someone's action, I did ad (N1) and that was a onetime onenight thing, so I think I can talk about it now. He did 2 actions N2, 0 actions N1. So I found it weird that mathisaac/scannose found something fishy about his actions. What do you think everybody???

I forgot why I think apw is town. I think my thought was "he was like towny. Bonus - he called me town first." and then I left it in a corner and never looked at it again. I think this was a very dumb thing to do. It looks like he got an inexperience challenge before the rude guy did, so he is probably really good at scum. But also I think that since there was a manipulative action on him he probably is not scum because I think that scum would want to manipulate him. Unless apw and Msv (Mathletesv) are scum partners and are like trying to get Msv town because apw town. But I think apw town anyways.
EDIT D3 START - Whoever killed apw is really mean.

I think Mathletesv is not scum. This is because fallinqrock- (Actually she is falliinqsnow- but her role was Falling Rock) seemed so super excited to hang him the second the musical witch (it feels weird thinking of him as a witch) said that he is scum. Why would she be so super excited to hang a scum? She is scum, so she wouldn't be excited right?

I think that ad333 is scum or it is scannose but not the both. Leaning towards ad scum because scannose really town. And because ad is anti-smiley. And because he has only 2 legs and no wings. Okay, maybe thats the real reason. But whatever, its weird, hes scum.

I think WisteriaV is town. She is actually scummy because apw said so but I think she is town because although D1 felt weird I think her posts earlier today come from a more towny perspective. This is also because there was this one post that was really good. It was voting mathisaac. Like from the Edgar Allan or whatever. But I upvoted it nevertheless. Besides that I think she is probably town after Day 2 of Mafia Game 257: Road Sign Role Madness (my English teacher said to underline Romeo and Juliet).
EDIT D3 START - Maybe wisteria is scum because apw died.

I think that scannose is town. I said one post was towny too. If you want to go find it that's a you problem. But scannose seems really good too so maybe they are a really good scum. Maybe they are that person ad said scientifically makes towny posts. Anyways I think that scannose is very towny. OH WAIT apw said he was spewed or skewed or s_ewed town because he said something about fakeclaims confusion. Maybe he is town. But also MaverickMan said that cai40 could conduct fake townslips as scum or something so maybe scannose can also do that! But also see ad, something is weird.

cai40 is probably town because of greencheck, same with cheesecake18.

bigmath is very obviously town (He is the only Batman in the game. I am calling him Batman because because Batman is very good at doctor AND is a vigilante! But if you wish I will say he is the only vigilante in the game.). Please do not correct this??
EDIT D3 START - I honestly find it really bad that he didnt die (not only because he killed mm but also because why would scum let a vig stay alive??). I think he is scummy.

EDIT D3 START - I think scummy people are viggymath and addy333

Sincerely,
Christopher (smh did you actually think i would name rev)

The idea is to draw with a pen/brush and use color fill as needed. This means that straight lines, right angles, perfect shapes, etc shouldn't really be in the drawing. However, if you've already submitted, it's fine as long as the straight/perfect parts are not the focus of the image.

Cross Here!: Day Action
- You have the ability to publish an event, a sample of which is here.
Sample Event Post wrote:
Event: Downtown Adventure


- All players must publicly submit a route in bold that goes from A1 to the red circle at C3. You can reduce your chances of your submission being missed by PMing us the link.
- You must make moves one cell down or to the right.
- If you fail to do so by EoD, you will be counted as having submitted the worst possible route, or one of the worst possible ones if there are multiple. (The worst route(s) will not be revealed.)
- Results, if any, will not be publicly revealed.
- Players may communicate about this event in all mod-given chats.

- Before 3 days into the day phase, submit a map to be used for the event. This map must include roads, road signs (including a deer crossing sign), a starting location, and an ending location. No more than half of possible routes from start to end may contain a deer crossing sign.
- Additionally, you must specify criteria for possible routes that the players submit. For example, in the sample, the criteria is "routes that only move down or right one space at a time."
- After the day ends, you will be provided with a list of all players who either posted a route crossing a road with a deer crossing sign, or failed to submit. (It is important to note that you will get more value out of your role if you maximize the number of players who do such.)
- You may not claim or hint at having info about the event.

Notes:
- Ideally suggest some flavor text. You may also request changes to the post format, instructions, etc.
- If you'd prefer, a map can be generated for you with similar quality to the sample.
- Here is a link to the sample map but without signs/coordinates: link. Here is a link to a Google Drawing with the map: link.

Dangerous Road: Night Action
- Target a player on the list mentioned above, killing them.
- This may only be used on odd nights.

Alerted Drivers: Night Action
- Target a player on the list mentioned above, protecting them from death.
- This may only be used on even nights.

Note: Under certain conditions, your role may be redacted when you die, causing only your alignment to flip. Do not claim or hint at this.

You can find the thread HERE (link).

[youtube]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RFCrJleggrI[/youtube]
[b]Cross Here!: Day Action[/b]
- You have the ability to publish an event, a sample of which is [hide=here.][quote=Sample Event Post]
[size=150][b][u]Event: Downtown Adventure[/u][/b][/size]

[img width="50"]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/847631095137697822/952663193350729779/Event_Map.png[/img]
- All players must publicly submit a route in [b]bold[/b] that goes from A1 to the red circle at C3. You can reduce your chances of your submission being missed by PMing us the link.
- You must make moves one cell down or to the right. 
- If you fail to do so by EoD, you will be counted as having submitted the worst possible route, or one of the worst possible ones if there are multiple. (The worst route(s) will not be revealed.)
- Results, if any, will not be publicly revealed.
- Players may communicate about this event in all mod-given chats.
[/quote][/hide] 
- Before 3 days into the day phase, submit a map to be used for the event. This map must include roads, road signs (including a deer crossing sign), a starting location, and an ending location. No more than half of possible routes from start to end may contain a deer crossing sign.
- Additionally, you must specify criteria for possible routes that the players submit. For example, in the sample, the criteria is "routes that only move down or right one space at a time."
- After the day ends, you will be provided with a list of all players who either posted a route crossing a road with a deer crossing sign, or failed to submit. (It is important to note that you will get more value out of your role if you maximize the number of players who do such.)
- [b]You may not claim or hint at having info about the event.[/b]

Notes:
- Ideally suggest some flavor text. You may also request changes to the post format, instructions, etc.
- If you'd prefer, a map can be generated for you with similar quality to the sample.
- Here is a link to the sample map but without signs/coordinates: [url=https://www.mipui.net/app/index.html?mid=ms0shdmpejf&secret=s0rryorpwpl]link.[/url] Here is a link to a Google Drawing with the map: [url=https://docs.google.com/drawings/d/1IYyCzAth9lzKV3XeLShKcFOWBmoLazIbUF2_0GtXrK0/edit]link.[/url]

[b]Dangerous Road: Night Action[/b]
- Target a player on the list mentioned above, killing them.
- This may only be used on odd nights.

[b]Alerted Drivers: Night Action[/b]
- Target a player on the list mentioned above, protecting them from death.
- This may only be used on even nights.

Note: Under certain conditions, your role may be redacted when you die, causing only your alignment to flip. Do not claim or hint at this.

You can find the thread [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c49h2910946_mafia_game_257_road_sign_role_madness]HERE[/url] (link).[/hide]

- Target a player, learning whether any of their actions this night did not have the intended effect. This is true if both of the following occur:

1) Target player properly submitted at least one action that was processed.
2) The intended effect of at least one of said processed actions did not occur for mechanical reasons.

[b]How Far Were We?: Night Action[/b]
- Target a dead player, learning if any of their actions on a night of your choosing did not have the intended effect.
- The criteria for an action not having the intended effect is the same as for "How Far Are We?"
- You are still counted as visiting the player, despite them being dead.

cai40, Runaway Truck Ramp
You are Runaway Truck Ramp, Town. You win when all the Mafia are eliminated.



In Case Of Emergency: Night Action
- Target a player, giving them access to the Runaway Truck Ramp. They will gain the Use and Repair actions below, with the following message given at the end of the night:
Quote:
You have been granted access to the Runaway Truck Ramp, with the following actions that you may use:

Use: Night Action
- Commute this night. This stops any actions that target you this night, but you may not use any other non-factional actions this night.
- This damages the Runaway Truck Ramp, preventing other Uses of it.

Repair: Night Action
- Help fix the Runaway Truck Ramp - it will become usable again after 2 total uses of this action across all players with access.
- If you do, you may not use other non-factional actions this night.
- This action can only be used when the Runaway Truck Ramp is damaged.
- If multiple players Use the Runaway Truck Ramp on the same night, only the Use by the player who had access to it the earliest will be successful.
- You start with access to the Runaway Truck Ramp, and unlike others who receive access, you may use other actions while you Use or Repair it.

Use: Night Action
- Commute this night. This stops any actions that target you this night.
- This damages the Runaway Truck Ramp, preventing other Uses of it.

Repair: Night Action
- Help fix the Runaway Truck Ramp - it will become usable again after 2 total uses of this action across all players with access.
- This action can only be used when the Runaway Truck Ramp is damaged.

Status Update: Passive
- After the Runaway Truck Ramp is Used or successfully Repaired, an announcement saying so will be made at the start of the next day.
- This effect persists after you die.

You can find the thread HERE (link).

- This may only trigger once, but you may submit every night until then.

- The player will receive the following message:
Quote:
A camera saw what happened to you last night.
- Unlock Zoom In for the next day only.

- This may only be used following the usage of All-Seeing in the night before.
- Choose one of the action types you saw targeting your target on the last night with All-Seeing, learning its name and description.
- At the end of this cycle's night, the player you targeted last night with All-Seeing will receive the following information about the same action:
Quote:

[Action Details]
- Unlock Replay for the next night only.

- If you use this, you may not use All-Seeing this night.

- Once per game, you will automatically be refunded a shot to be used the next night (ignoring the above restriction), if a shot from this action does not go through due to not meeting its conditions. This refunded shot must satisfy the same conditions.

[b]Alerted: Passive[/b]
- After 5 Town die, the requirement for [b]Crush[/b] to succeed increase by 1 visit.

[b]The next post in this PM will contain important details about the Mafia, including your teammates' identities and their roles.[/b]

[b]Nightkill[/b] - Compulsive Night Action - Assigned
- Kill a player. (Note: Unlike with other actions, you may use this on the same player multiple times in a row.)

[b]Backup: Immediate Action[/b]
- Choose a dead Mafia member. Gain a factional 1-shot copy of each of their abilities (they can be used by any wolf).

Note: Assigned means that the action is performed by one Mafia member, and thus can be tracked, watched, roleblocked, etc.

[quote]A camera saw what happened to you last night.[/quote]
- Unlock [b]Zoom In[/b] for the next night only.

[b]Zoom In: Night Action[/b]
- This may only be used following the usage of [b]All-Seeing[/b] in the night before.
- Choose one of the action types you saw targeting your target on the last night with [b]All-Seeing[/b], learning its name and description.
- At the end of this cycle's night, the player you targeted last night with [b]All-Seeing[/b] will receive the following information about the same action:
[quote]Further investigation has revealed one action taken on you on night X:

[Action Details]
[/quote]
- If you use this, you may not use [b]All-Seeing[/b] this night.
